monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Mapun / Sulu folklore traditions, recorded in colonial-era accounts
monsters.passive_ability: Astral Detachment — Once per battle at low HP, leaves a spirit double that absorbs one direct attack and allows Berbalang to counter with Underworld damage.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Grave-Echo Feast — Sends astral jaws through the ground to strike one enemy, dealing Underworld damage and healing if the target is affected by Fear, Doom, or Despair.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Soul-Body Divide — Splits into flesh and spirit forms for two turns; the spirit casts debuffs while the body attacks marked targets.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Soul Drain, Fear Cry, Doom Mark, Spirit Leak, Shadow Step, Grave Bite, Dark Pulse, Weaken, Silence, Curse, Life Siphon, Hidden, Spirit Ward, Counter Ready, Despair Wail
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires resolving a burial taboo and calming the ancestral dead
monsters.habitat: Graveyard, Spirit Threshold, Limestone Cave, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Burial flowers, black rice, incense, water for the dead, remembered names
monsters.lore_and_backstory: The Berbalang is one of the strangest and most unsettling Nilalang in the ALAMAT Codex. Elders in the southern islands speak of beings connected to graves, hunger, and spirit projection—creatures whose true danger is not merely tooth or claw, but the ability to separate presence from body. In ALAMAT, the Berbalang is born where burial rites are broken and the dead are treated as refuse instead of ancestors. This Berbalang is not recruited through combat alone. The player must return stolen grave markers, restore the names of the forgotten dead, and discover why a clan of mourners abandoned an old cemetery. Once the dead are acknowledged, the Berbalang's hunger changes direction: it feeds on corruption clinging to restless souls rather than on the innocent. In battle, it is a dangerous summoner-debuffer that fights through astral echoes, split forms, and grave-born fear.
monsters.folklore_summary: Berbalang accounts are commonly associated with Cagayan Sulu, now Mapun, and are described in published folklore as ghoul-like beings connected with eating human flesh and unusual supernatural powers. Much popular detail traces back to an 1896 report by E. F. Skertchly.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT interprets Berbalang as an Underworld Nilalang tied to neglected burials, astral projection, and the responsibility to remember the dead.
monsters.physical_description: A gaunt humanoid with ash-gray skin, luminous hollow eyes, long fingers, rib-like spirit markings, and a second translucent astral body slightly misaligned behind it. The mouth glows with blue corpse-fire rather than gore.
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred cleansing, Sun skills, remembered-name rituals, bell wards, anti-summon effects
monsters.resistances: Underworld, Shadow, Spirit Leak, Fear, Doom, poison
monsters.npc_related: Apo Samad the grave keeper; Mira the child who remembers names; Imam-Lorekeeper Halun
monsters.boss_variant: Berbalang Twin-Soul — body and spirit have separate HP bars and linked curses.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Gutóm na Anino — a ravenous astral ghoul that devours memory fragments.
monsters.divine_variant: Tagapag-alaga ng Libingan — grave guardian that protects ancestral names.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A hollow double-voice, one close and one far underground, whispering forgotten names.
monsters.drop_items: Grave Ash, Astral Fang, Name-Stone Fragment, Blue Corpse-Fire
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bone Talisman, Bell, Spirit Mask, Ancestral Charm, Relic Weapon
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Berbalang inspired by Philippine folklore: gaunt graveyard spirit-ghoul, ash-gray skin, hollow blue glowing eyes, long fingers, translucent astral double offset behind the body, underworld blue fire aura, respectful dark fantasy, transparent background, readable top-down chibi RPG silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Berbalang from southern Philippine folklore, gaunt humanoid ghoul with ash skin, hollow luminous eyes, astral spirit double behind, cemetery mist, blue corpse-fire glow, eerie but not graphic, painterly RPG bestiary style.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Berbalang's spirit peels away from its body, dives underground as blue fire, ghostly jaws erupt under the target, then the body and spirit snap back together with a grave-bell echo.
monsters.design_notes: Treat with respect around burial themes. Avoid sensational gore; emphasize memory, graves, and astral horror.

monsters.passive_ability: Sleep Paralysis — Enemies affected by Sleep, Fear, or Slow have reduced evasion and cannot benefit from Haste while Batibat is active.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Weight of the Old Post — Crushes one enemy with nightmare pressure, dealing Dream damage and applying Sleep or Slow.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Housepost Nightmare — Turns the battlefield into a dream-house for two turns; sleeping enemies take extra damage and allies gain Spirit Defense.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Sleep, Fear Cry, Dream Pulse, Root Prison, Slow, Curse, Spirit Leak, Weaken, Fortify, Regeneration, Dark Pulse, Silence, Shielded, Despair Wail, Cleanse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires returning a displaced tree spirit to a living grove
monsters.habitat: Ancestral House, Balete Grove, Old Chapel Ruins, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Wood oil, woven sleeping mat, lullaby, clean pillow cloth, tree sap bowl
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Batibat is feared as a nightmare spirit, often connected to old trees and house posts. In ALAMAT, a Batibat is created when a living tree spirit is cut down without apology, ritual, or replanting, then trapped inside the beams of a house. It becomes heavy with grief and anger, pressing nightmares onto those who sleep near its wood. The player encounters this Batibat in a family home where generations have suffered the same choking dream. The solution is not to destroy the house, but to learn which tree was taken, plant its descendant, and perform a waking ritual so the spirit can leave the post. Once trusted, Batibat becomes a powerful dream controller who weakens enemies through Sleep, Slow, and pressure. Its story teaches that even shelter can become haunted when made from a life taken without gratitude.
monsters.folklore_summary: Batibat is described in Ilocano folklore as a vengeful, tree-dwelling female spirit associated with nightmares, sleep paralysis, and suffocation during sleep, especially when its tree has been cut and made into a house post.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT reimagines Batibat as a displaced tree-mother spirit whose nightmare attacks come from grief, homelessness, and broken respect between humans and trees.
monsters.physical_description: A massive ancient female tree-spirit with bark-like folds, heavy limbs, sleepy glowing eyes, mossy hair, root fingers, and a half-wooden lower body merging into a house post. Dream smoke coils around her shoulders like a blanket.
monsters.weaknesses: Sun, Sacred lullabies, wakefulness rituals, axe-oath reparations, fire if corrupted
monsters.resistances: Dream, Sleep, Fear, Root, physical knockback, Underworld pressure
monsters.npc_related: Lola Ines the dream sufferer; Jun the carpenter's grandson; Apo Raniag the tree planter
monsters.boss_variant: Batibat ng Haliging Itim — traps the party in rotating nightmare rooms.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Bangungot Batibat — drains HP from sleeping enemies every turn.
monsters.divine_variant: Ina ng Mapayapang Tulog — guardian of safe sleep and restored homes.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A low lullaby that becomes a creaking house beam and a breath held too long.
monsters.drop_items: Nightmare Bark, Dream Resin, Old Housepost Splinter, Lullaby Thread
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Herbal Bundle, Woven Garb, Bell, Spirit Mask
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Batibat inspired by Ilocano folklore: massive ancient tree-woman nightmare spirit, bark-like folds, moss hair, glowing sleepy eyes, root fingers, body merging with housepost, dream smoke aura, transparent background, readable chibi RPG proportions without ridicule.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Batibat, Philippine nightmare tree spirit, large ancient female forest-housepost being, bark-textured skin, moss hair, weary glowing eyes, dream mist, old wooden house atmosphere, tragic and intimidating RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Batibat presses both wooden palms downward, the battlefield dims into a sleeping room, a giant shadow-housepost falls as dream smoke coils around enemies, applying Sleep and Slow.
monsters.design_notes: Avoid mocking body shape. Focus on ancient-tree heaviness and nightmare pressure, not caricature.

monsters.passive_ability: Laughing Panic — Gains attack after taking damage, but loses accuracy when Confused, Taunted, or tricked.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Carabao-Hurling Strength — Lifts and throws a huge boulder-like force at one enemy, dealing heavy Physical/Earth damage and pushing the target back.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Bungisngis Belly Laugh — A thunderous laugh shakes the field, lowering enemy defense but giving clever enemies a chance to apply Confusion back to Bungisngis.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heavy Strike, Earth Slam, Guard Break, Taunt, Reckless Charge, Stone Throw, Fortify, Roar, Stomp, Counter Ready, Weaken, Fear Cry, Haste, Protect Ally, Confuse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium / Trust Difficulty: Requires winning a trick contest without humiliating it
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Limestone Cave, Bamboo Thicket, Market Town outskirts
monsters.weather_preference: Clear, Cloudy, Heat Haze, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Roasted root crops, coconut, banana bunch, carabao milk, sweet rice cakes
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Bungisngis is remembered as a laughing one-eyed giant: immensely strong, frightening at first glance, but often foolish enough to be outwitted by smaller heroes and animals. In ALAMAT, Bungisngis belongs to a family of earth-fed giants who once helped clear boulders from mountain paths. Over time, people stopped asking politely and began tricking them into labor, so many Bungisngis grew defensive, loud, and easily embarrassed. The player finds this Bungisngis blocking a road not out of malice, but because merchants mocked its eye and stole its food. To earn trust, the player must beat it in a contest of jokes, strength, and kindness: make it laugh, return its stolen meal, and refuse to shame it after winning. In combat, Bungisngis is a simple but powerful bruiser. It hits hard, disrupts defenses, and becomes dangerous when angered, but clever enemies can exploit its poor focus.
monsters.folklore_summary: Bungisngis is a one-eyed giant in Philippine folklore, especially associated with Bataan and Batangas. It is described as always laughing, very strong, but easily outwitted in folk tales.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT makes Bungisngis a comic but sympathetic earth giant whose laughter hides embarrassment and a desire for honest friendship.
monsters.physical_description: A broad one-eyed giant with tan-brown skin, huge grin with tusk-like teeth, oversized hands, thick legs, simple woven belt, stone bracelets, and a single expressive eye. Its body language is playful and loud rather than purely monstrous.
monsters.weaknesses: Confusion, Trickster skills, precision attacks to the eye, Dream illusions, speed-based evasion
monsters.resistances: Earth, physical knockback, fear, root, heavy impact damage
monsters.npc_related: Kiko the monkey-trickster; Mang Pilo the merchant; Alon the carabao herder
monsters.boss_variant: Bungisngis Hari ng Tawa — gains strength every time any unit misses.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Ngising Bato — stone-skinned giant whose laughter causes Despair.
monsters.divine_variant: Masayang Bantay-Bundok — joyful mountain helper with golden stone bracelets.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A booming giggle that turns into a mountain-shaking belly laugh.
monsters.drop_items: Giant Tooth Chip, Laughing Stone, Woven Giant Belt, Boulder Core
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Relic Weapon, Woven Garb, Stone Charm, Bell
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Bungisngis inspired by Philippine folklore: friendly-frightening one-eyed giant, huge grin, tusk-like teeth, big hands, tan-brown skin, woven belt, stone bracelets, playful stance, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Bungisngis, a one-eyed laughing giant from Philippine folklore, huge expressive eye, broad grin, tusks, warm earthy skin, mountain forest background, comic yet powerful RPG creature design.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Bungisngis laughs so hard the ground shakes, grabs a huge stone, spins clumsily, then hurls it forward with enormous force as dust bursts outward.
monsters.design_notes: Keep the tone adventurous and slightly humorous. Avoid making the creature stupid-only; give it emotional dignity.

monsters.province_origin: Siquijor, Bohol, Cebu, Leyte, and folk-healing/witchcraft traditions
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Visayan and wider Philippine witchcraft folklore
monsters.passive_ability: Insect Familiar — Poison, Rot, and Marked effects applied by Mambabarang last one extra turn unless cleansed by Sacred or Herbal skills.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Hair-Tied Hex — Marks one enemy using a ritual thread; insects swarm the target, applying Poison and Spirit Leak.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Hive Inside the Doll — Creates a curse effigy that stores damage for two turns, then releases it as Poison/Shadow damage to the marked enemy.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Poison Fang, Curse, Spirit Leak, Rot, Mark Prey, Bug Swarm, Silence, Weaken, Dark Pulse, Hex Thread, Smoke Veil, Fear Cry, Cleanse, Barrier, Life Siphon
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires breaking a revenge cycle and healing the curse victim
monsters.habitat: Market Town, Ancestral House, Bamboo Thicket, Corrupted Wilds, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Bitter herbs, beeswax, black thread, honeycomb, clean needle
monsters.lore_and_backstory: The Mambabarang is feared as a curse-worker who commands insects, worms, or crawling things to harm a chosen victim. In ALAMAT, not every Mambabarang begins as evil. Some are ritual specialists who learned to speak with small lives—ants, beetles, moths, and flies—then turned that knowledge toward revenge when justice failed. This Mambabarang once protected farmers by driving pests away from crops, but corruption fed on resentment after a powerful family escaped punishment for poisoning a river. The player must not simply defeat the Mambabarang; they must expose the original crime, heal the curse victim, and convince the insects to return to balance. Once bonded, Mambabarang becomes a technical shaman who wins battles through poison, delayed curses, marks, and spirit leakage.
monsters.folklore_summary: Mambabarang is commonly described in Philippine folklore as a sorcerer or witch who uses insects, worms, or similar creatures to afflict victims through ritual magic, often involving personal items such as hair.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Mambabarang as a dangerous but understandable curse-worker whose power over insects can be redirected from revenge toward protection and cleansing corrupted infestations.
monsters.physical_description: A thin ritualist in dark woven clothing, face partially covered by a beaded veil, carrying a small bamboo curse tube, black thread wrapped around fingers, with moths, ants, and beetles moving in controlled spiral patterns around the body.
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred cleansing, Fire purification, honest confession quests, anti-poison herbs, bell disruption
monsters.resistances: Poison, Rot, Shadow, Spirit Leak, insects, curse reflection
monsters.npc_related: Manang Talia the healer; Dario the poisoned fisherman; Judge Amihan the corrupt official
monsters.boss_variant: Mambabarang Reyna ng Pukyutan — summons multiple insect effigies and spreads poison between allies.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Salot na Mambabarang — loses human restraint and turns entire fields into crawling curse zones.
monsters.divine_variant: Tagapangalaga ng Maliliit na Buhay — protector of insects and harvest balance.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A low whispered chant layered with the rising buzz of insects.
monsters.drop_items: Black Ritual Thread, Insect Wax, Curse Tube Splinter, Bitter Herb Bundle
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Herbal Bundle, Spirit Mask, Bone Talisman, Tattoo Mark, Instrument
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Mambabarang inspired by Philippine folklore: dark-clothed ritual curse-worker, beaded veil, bamboo curse tube, black thread around fingers, controlled swarm of tiny insects, purple-green poison aura, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Mambabarang insect hex-weaver, Filipino folklore-inspired ritualist with dark woven garments, beaded veil, black thread, bamboo tube, moths and beetles circling, eerie green-purple lighting, painterly RPG character art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Mambabarang ties a black thread around a glowing hair strand, insects spiral into a curse doll, the doll cracks open, and a swarm shoots toward the marked enemy with poison glyphs.
monsters.design_notes: Do not portray as simple evil witch. Link gameplay to justice, revenge, and ecological balance of small creatures.

monsters.region_of_origin: Visayas and Mindanao; wider Philippine folklore
monsters.province_origin: Eastern Visayas, Cebu, Bohol, Leyte, Samar, Mindanao accounts
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Visayan folklore; related Hiligaynon Amamayong comparisons in some accounts
monsters.passive_ability: Backward Gait — Gains evasion against frontal attacks and deals bonus damage to targets suffering Marked or Blind.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Shadow-Suckle Bite — Attacks the target's shadow instead of body, dealing Shadow damage and applying Weakened or Bleed.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Ear-Clap Vanish — Claps its huge ears to create a nauseating shockwave, becomes Hidden, and may Blind all enemies.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Bite, Quick Claw, Shadow Step, Blind, Mark Prey, Ambush, Fear Cry, Poison Fang, Haste, Evasive Step, Blood Drain, Weaken, Counter Ready, Dark Pulse, Smoke Veil
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires freeing it from a cruel Sigbinan pact
monsters.habitat: Bamboo Thicket, Ancestral House, Market Town outskirts, Corrupted Wilds, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Shadow-safe lantern, goat milk, black cloth, sweet banana, clay jar charm
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Sigbin is whispered of as a strange night beast: goat-like yet wrong, walking backward with its head lowered, able to hide from human sight and attack the shadow rather than the flesh. In ALAMAT, Sigbin are not naturally evil. Many are bound as familiars by families who inherit forbidden contracts. The creature's hunger is shaped by the master who commands it. This Sigbin is found trapped inside a clay jar, released only to steal shadows for a corrupt household. To gain its trust, the player must break the family pact, return stolen shadows to children, and teach the Sigbin to follow its own will. Once bonded, it becomes a fast assassin and scout, specializing in stealth, blind effects, and attacks that bypass ordinary armor by striking the target's shadow.
monsters.folklore_summary: Sigbin or Sigben is described in Philippine mythology as a nocturnal creature that may suck blood from shadows, walk backward with its head lowered, become invisible, possess large ears it can clap, and be kept by certain families known as Sigbinan.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Sigbin as a shadow-beast often exploited as a familiar. Its trust story focuses on freeing it from inherited cruelty and redirecting its stealth toward protection.
monsters.physical_description: A low, backward-walking beast resembling a hornless goat-dog with oversized clapping ears, a flexible whip-like tail, lowered head tucked near its hind legs, shadowy paws, glowing yellow eyes, and a smoky odor aura.
monsters.weaknesses: Sun, Sacred bells, reveal skills, honest-lantern items, frontal traps if baited correctly
monsters.resistances: Shadow, Beast, Blind, evasion penalties, back attacks, fear
monsters.npc_related: Nena the shadowless child; Don Arcadio the Sigbinan heir; Pilo the jar maker
monsters.boss_variant: Sigbin ng Pitong Banga — seven clay jars release shadow clones each turn.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Aninong Sigbin — becomes almost invisible and feeds on party luck.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay-Anino — freed shadow guardian that protects children at night.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A wet goat-like bleat followed by two sharp ear-claps and a low growl from behind.
monsters.drop_items: Shadow Fur, Clay Jar Seal, Ear-Leather, Black Tail Fiber
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Spirit Mask, Bell, Bone Talisman, Anting-Anting, Tattoo Mark
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Sigbin inspired by Philippine folklore: strange hornless goat-dog shadow beast walking backward, oversized clapping ears, lowered head, flexible tail, yellow glowing eyes, smoky black aura, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Sigbin, Philippine shadow-feeding beast, hornless goat-like body, huge ears, lowered backward posture, glowing yellow eyes, clay jar and moonlit bamboo background, eerie but animal-like RPG creature design.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Sigbin claps its oversized ears, vanishes into a black ripple, races backward across the ground, bites the enemy's shadow, and reappears behind the target with a whip-tail snap.
monsters.design_notes: Make the Sigbin unsettling and agile, not just a dog or goat. Emphasize backward movement and shadow mechanics.
